title: DeBriefed 19 June 2026: Bonn talks end in ‘gridlock’ | Energy’s ‘new era’ | Oceans in climate negotiations - Carbon Brief
gist: Weekly climate-and-energy digest: UN Bonn talks collapsed into gridlock and were deferred to COP31 Turkey; a US-Iran interim deal reopening the strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices down with the IEA now forecasting a 2027 oil glut; and oil majors are retreating from renewables while UK EVs are shown to save drivers ~£3bn a year.

insights.claim: A US-Iran interim agreement to halt the war and reopen the strait of Hormuz has pushed oil prices down and begun easing the global energy crisis triggered by the conflict.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Crude downside catalyst confirmed; watch for follow-through if the ceasefire holds, but high fuel prices may persist near-term.
insights.quote: high fuel prices will “likely outlast the Iran war”.

insights.claim: The International Energy Agency has forecast a glut of oil emerging in 2027 if the US-Iran peace deal holds, enabling countries to rebuild strategic reserves.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: A forecast oil glut implies medium-term price pressure and a window for state reserve restocking — bearish for crude into 2027.
insights.quote: the International Energy Agency (IEA) has forecast a “glut of oil” emerging next year, if the peace deal holds.

insights.claim: Energy-industry analysts now treat Hormuz chokepoint risk as a structural, repricable hazard rather than a transient geopolitical headline after more than three months of blockage.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Persistent Hormuz risk premium argues for a structurally higher tail-risk component in Gulf energy and shipping/insurance pricing.
insights.quote: the war “showed the oil and gas industry that Hormuz risk is no longer just a geopolitical headline”.

themes.theme: Oil majors retreating from clean energy

insights.claim: Equinor has dropped a renewable-energy target and scaled back clean investments, while Shell is selling off its offshore wind assets.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Signals oil majors reallocating away from renewables — bearish read-through for offshore wind supply chains, watch for asset sales creating buy-side opportunities.
insights.quote: Equinor has dropped a renewable-energy target and scaled back clean investments, while another Reuters story noted that Shell is selling off its offshore wind assets.

insights.claim: UN climate talks in Bonn ended in gridlock with both adaptation finance and emissions cuts subject to 'rule 16', pushing all work to the COP31 summit in Turkey with no agreement reached.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Stalled multilateral process means weaker near-term policy tailwinds for climate-exposed assets; momentum shifting outside formal diplomacy.
insights.quote: much of the focus in Bonn had “shift[ed] to efforts outside diplomatic talks – raising questions about the future of global climate negotiations”.

insights.claim: The 1.5C target is increasingly being challenged within the UN process and the IPCC's role in an upcoming global progress assessment remains controversial, amid warnings of attacks on science by a small group of fossil-fuel interests.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Erosion of the 1.5C anchor weakens the policy basis for aggressive decarbonisation timelines investors have priced in.

insights.claim: UK electric vehicles (2m+ BEVs, 1m PHEVs, 100,000 electric vans) are saving drivers around £3bn a year, more than £1,100 a year per BEV driver, even as the government considers watering down EV sales targets.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Quantified consumer fuel savings is a pro-EV data point; potential dilution of UK EV mandates is a policy risk for the sector.
insights.quote: more than £1,100 a year in fuel costs for each BEV driver in the UK

insights.claim: Ocean finance currently makes up less than 1% of all climate finance, even though three-quarters of the latest round of national climate plans (NDCs) mention oceans.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: A sub-1% allocation versus rising NDC ocean commitments flags blue-carbon and ocean-adaptation as an underfunded, potentially expanding finance niche.
insights.quote: ocean finance, which currently makes up less than 1% of all climate finance

insights.claim: The Trump administration is abandoning a plan to dismantle a $368m ocean monitoring system after bipartisan backlash on Capitol Hill.
insights.speaker: Josh Gabbatiss
insights.so_what: Reversal signals limits to US science-funding cuts where bipartisan support exists — relevant to climate-data and observation contractors.
